content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                Date : 6-29-77


                                                         M-77-229

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject: Modification of memo concerning advice to Fred Furth.


       In the process of researching another issue, the staff had
the opportunity to reconsider the memorandum dated November
22, 1976, relating advice provided to Fred Furth. The staff
continues to endorse the end result of the advice provided
to Mr. Furth. However, we wish to modify the memorandum
on which that advice was based to the extent that it con-
cluded that "regular elections" within the meaning of Elec-
tions Code Section 26 were not "elections" within the mean-
ing of Government Code Section 82022 which provides:

       Election means any primary, general, special or
       recall election held in this state...

       As used in this section, the term "general" does not refer
exclusively to the statewide general election as referred
to in Elections Code Section 20. Rather, it refers to any
general election held within the state, i.e., regular elections
as used within the meaning of Elections Code Section 26.

       Thus, the local general city elections held in March and
April are "general" elections within the meaning of Section
82022 even though they are not the "general" elections re-
ferred to in Elections Code Section 20.

       The term "primary" as used in Section 82022 refers to the
types of primary elections defined in Elections Code Sections
21 and 23.


TP:jo

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                Date : Nov. 22, 1976

                                                        77
                                                        M-229

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Mike Baker

Subject: Election for Chairmanship of State Democratic Central Committee.

Susan Reilly called on November 18, 1976, on behalf of Fred
Furth, who is a candidate for the Chairmanship of the State
Democratic Central Committee. She asked whether he is required to file campaign statements in connection with that
race under the Act.

The matter was taken up at the Opinion Request Meeting on
November 19, and it was concluded that this race is not an
"election" within the meaning of Section 82023 and that the
Chairmanship is not an "elective office" within the meaning
of Section 82023, and therefore that Furth is not a candidate
within the meaning of Section 82007 and has no filing obligation.

The procedure for electing the Chairman of the Democratic
State Central Committee is established in Elections Code
Sections 8661-79. Those provisions provide that the Committee shall meet in Sacramento on the fourth Sunday in
January following a General Election. The members of the
State Committee are certain elected officials, victors in
Democratic primaries, and persons appointed by such elected
officials and candidates. These delegates elect the Chairman. There is a question of whether this procedure would be
deemed an "election" within the meaning of the Elections
Code. Even if it is, however, it would be a "regular election" within the meaning of Elections Code Section 29 because
it would be "an election, the specific time for the holding
of which is prescribed by law." The definition of "election"
in Section 82022 of the Act includes primary, general, special
and recall elections, but not "regular elections." Therefore,
even if this procedure is an election within the meaning of
the Elections Code, it is not an election within the meaning
of the Act.

MB:50
